summary: This is a very brief correction note to a previous post. Joel points out that the compression algorithm commonly known as 'LZW' should more properly be called 'ZLW' or 'Ziv-Lempel-Welch,' since the original paper credited Ziv as the primary author. The post is only 35 words and serves as a minor factual addendum rather than a substantive essay.
key_points: The compression algorithm commonly called LZW should properly credit Ziv first, as he was the primary author on the original paper
Joel demonstrates attention to proper academic attribution even in casual blog posts
money_quotes: Although the compression algorithm is commonly called "LZW," the original paper was credited to Ziv first, so I think it should properly be referred to as Ziv-Lempel-Welch or ZLW.
key_insight: Proper attribution matters: the LZW compression algorithm should credit Ziv first, as the original paper did.
